I've reverse engineered Claude's new feature so you don't have to
Woke up today to the news that Claude Code dropped a new command called /simplify. Its purpose is to:
Boris Cherny
@bcherny
·
Feb 28
Replying to
@bcherny
/simplify Use parallel agents to improve code quality, tune code efficiency, and ensure CLAUDE.md compliance. Usage: "hey claude make this code change then run /simplify"
Pretty cool feature. The main issue is I stopped using Claude Code ages ago, so I reverse engineered it. Now you can use it with whatever coding agent you want.
Under the hood, it’s basically a master /simplify prompt plus three sub-agent prompts generated for parallel reviews Master prompt (/simplify). Kicks off the review. It triggers a review of your code changes, and then it kicks off three parallel reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency), applies worthwhile fixes, and returns a concise summary.
Code reuse agent: Reviews the diff to find duplicated or hand-rolled logic, searches for existing helpers and utilities in the codebase, and recommends reusing those instead of adding new duplicate code.
Code quality agent: Reviews your diff for maintainability issues like redundant state, parameter sprawl, copy-paste variants, leaky abstractions, and stringly-typed patterns, and points out what should be refactored.
Efficiency review agent: Reviews your diff for performance and resource issues like redundant/dead code, missed concurrency, hot-path bloat, TOCTOU-style prechecks, memory leaks, and overly broad operations, and reports what should be optimized.
The nicest part is that it uses the real diff in the prompts, so it stays focused on what you changed. Enjoy.
